title: Cedarwood and vetiver cologne balm
subtitle: A dry, earthy woody fragrance in a portable solid balm format
excerpt: A solid perfume balm scented with cedarwood, vetiver, and bergamot essential oils. A dry, woodsy cologne-register fragrance. Applies easily to pulse points; the beeswax carrier makes it travel-friendly and long-lasting on warm skin.
sourceNotes: Solid perfume formulation: 30% beeswax to 70% jojoba base. Essential oil usage rate for leave-on skin products: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014). Cedarwood, vetiver, and bergamot at combined 2.5% are within safe limits for a leave-on product applied to pulse points.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Total essential oil at 2.5% of formula weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 1 hour to set before capping.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store in the tin away from heat. Keeps 12 months. The bergamot top note fades first; cedarwood and vetiver are both stable base notes that persist. Discard if the jojoba smells rancid.

glossaryTerms.definition: A preparation containing no water phase. Solid perfume keeps 12 months without a preservative.
The temperature at which the wax component fully liquefies. A 30% beeswax formula sets firm at room temperature but softens at skin temperature.
Apply a small amount to the inside of the forearm and leave for 24 to 48 hours before applying to pulse points.

content.text: Cedarwood and vetiver are both base notes with significant longevity on warm skin. Together they produce a dry, earthy, woody character in the cologne register: not sweet, not floral. Bergamot adds a brief citrus brightness on first application that fades within the hour, leaving the woody base. The solid balm format is particularly well-suited to base-note fragrances because the beeswax carrier slows the initial fragrance release compared to a spray or oil; the scent builds gradually as skin heat warms the balm. At 2.5% combined essential oil content, this is an
leave-on product within the standard skin-safe range. The
of the beeswax keeps the balm firm in a pocket or bag.

Melt beeswax and jojoba oil together in the double boiler over low heat. Remove from heat, cool to 55°C, add all three essential oils, stir to combine, and pour immediately into the small tin. Allow 1 hour to set before capping.
on the inside of the forearm for 24 to 48 hours before applying to pulse points.
Rub a fingertip gently on the surface of the tin to pick up a small amount of balm. Apply to wrists, neck, or inside the elbows. Body heat releases the fragrance gradually over several hours.
Store in the tin away from heat and sunlight. Keeps 12 months. Discard if the jojoba develops a rancid smell.
